reviews.review_msg: Emily is extremely kind and very approachable. She genuinely wants you to do well. The class was straightforward and depended on group projects. Her lectures aren't the most helpful for her exams though.

Emily is one of the most passionate and kind professors I have had during my time at SFU. Her classes aren't necessarily easy and you need to work hard if you want to achieve a good grade, but she's always willing to help out when needed.

While she is nice, she is clearly and inexperienced prof. Her teaching style isn't very effective. Takes marks for participation but doesn't really teach anything in class (no examples she says are used in the exams). Very broad description of group projects given.

Probably the best professor I've had in Beedie. She is very sweet and clearly cares about her students and their learning experience. Overall very chill and approachable. Genuinely wants you to understand the content she teaches and is clearly passionate. Her exams and assignments are fair, and she's very understanding of students.

Great professor, she is young so very relatable to students. The exams are very fair and nothing out of the blue, just go over the textbook. Go to her for help with the group project, not going was a mistake our group made. Overall great class and you learn a lot about how businesses are conducted in nations other than Canada.

Emily is very nice and approachable, but I feel like she's not very experienced with teaching yet. Her lectures are kind of dry and I didn't learn much. Midterm is easy if you study from the textbook.

I enjoyed taking the course with Emily so much! Whenever I have problems about the course content or the group project, Emily is always there to help me. The course was simple: 1 mid-term, 1 final, 2 discussion questions, and a group project. The group project was highly related to the course content, was helpful to better understand the content!

This class is all about discussion and participation. If you follow the discussions you'll guarantee a B easily. Emily is a super nice person and makes the class as engaging as possible. Theres no textbook but she makes sure that the course content covers what you need to know. Very chill, easy to understand, down to earth and passionate

Right off the bat, she's not a set professor for SFU but a Ph.D. student in her final year that just teaches marketing. That being said, she is incredibly passionate about the topic and makes an effort to ensure understanding by every single student. She makes an active effort to know students by name and is readily and responsive to queries

Emily is the best prof I've had at SFU. Her lectures are very engaging and she is clearly passionate about marketing. Maybe it's because the class was only 40 people, but she made the effort to learn everyone's name and was friendly with all of us, which made it feel more like a peer-to-peer relationship, rather than teacher-to-student.
